pick.rationale: Arizona enters as the clear favorite in this Sweet 16 matchup, leveraging their #3 national ranking and massive 12-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over Arkansas's #17-20 profile. The Wildcats' elite defense ranked 3rd nationally should contain Arkansas's top-5 offense better than the Razorbacks' 50th-ranked defense can handle Arizona's balanced top-10 attack. While John Calipari's SEC Tournament championship gives Arkansas momentum and tournament experience, Arizona's 31-2 record, Big 12 Tournament title, and historical 1-seed success rate in the Sweet 16 provide a decisive edge in this West Region contest.

reasoningStep.title: Arizona vs Arkansas Sweet 16 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Arizona holds substantial advantages in efficiency metrics, defensive quality, and overall season dominance that should overcome Arkansas's elite offense and Calipari's tournament experience.
reasoningStep.evidence: Arizona ranks #3 nationally (KenPom/Torvik) with 37.56 adjusted efficiency margin vs Arkansas at #17-20 with 25.43 margin. Arizona's defense (#3 nationally) edges Arkansas's offense (#5) in the key matchup battle, while Arkansas's 50th-ranked defense is vulnerable to Arizona's top-10 offense. Historical 1-seed vs 4-seed Sweet 16 matchups favor the 1-seed approximately 65-70% of the time. Both teams won conference tournaments, but Arizona's 31-2 record reflects greater season-long dominance than Arkansas's 25-8 mark.
